reasoning: Since its inception, Bitcoin has historically closed higher than it started in all post-halving years (2013, 2017, 2021). The search results indicate strong fundamental and macroeconomic support, including pro-crypto policies from the new US administration, ongoing rate cuts, and large-scale institutional adoption via ETFs. Although the market exhibited some weakness and geopolitical sensitivity in mid-2025, it appeared to find strong support near $100,000, recently recovering from an ATH of $112,000. Major institutional forecasts generally predict end-of-year values well above the target, typically between $130,000 and $200,000. While a crypto market correction of 15-20% in the last months of the year could drop prices below the $93,429.20 threshold, the combination of historically strong Q4 performance during bull cycles and current positive catalysts makes a YES outcome highly likely.
